title: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Campaign Monitor and Constant Contact. See pricing, features, and ratings to find the best email marketing tool for your business.
h1: Campaign Monitor vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Campaign Monitor and Constant Contact are both established email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Campaign Monitor excels for agencies and multi-brand operations, while Constant Contact is designed for nonprofits and local businesses looking for integrated marketing tools.
choose_a_if: You manage multiple brands or client accounts and need multi-brand management capabilities
Advanced automation through journey designer and smart segmentation is critical to your strategy
You prefer a platform with higher user ratings on G2
choose_b_if: You need integrated SMS marketing and social media advertising features
You run an e-commerce business and require built-in shopping cart capabilities
You're a nonprofit or local business seeking event management tools

faq.q: Is Campaign Monitor better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: Campaign Monitor edges ahead for agencies and teams managing multiple brands, with stronger automation and segmentation tools. However, Constant Contact is better if you need SMS, social ads, and e-commerce features integrated into one platform. The choice depends on your specific business needs rather than overall superiority.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Campaign Monitor or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Pricing is nearly identical, with Campaign Monitor starting at $11/month and Constant Contact at $12/month. Both platforms offer no free tier, so you'll need to commit to paid plans immediately. The actual cost difference will depend on your subscriber count and feature selections.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Campaign Monitor?
faq.a: Yes, switching between platforms is possible. Both tools typically support list export functions, allowing you to download your contacts and import them into Campaign Monitor. However, you'll need to rebuild automation sequences and redesign templates, as these features don't transfer directly between platforms.
